combinedCode:
# ── BFS Maze — drag to build walls, click to explore ─────────────────────────
# Interactive mode only — there is no algorithm to trace.
# After clicking Analyze, click "Finish & Interact" to start.
#
#  • Drag any wall block:
#      → onto an empty board cell  → it stays there
#      → onto an occupied cell     → it snaps back to where it started
#      → outside the board         → it disappears
#  • Drag the orange source block (left of board):
#      → a new wall block is spawned and follows your mouse
#      → drop it on an empty board cell to place it
#  • Click any empty board cell → BFS fills reachable cells (shows distance)
#  • Click the Clear button → reset fill, click again for a new run
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

import random
from collections import deque

# ── Board constants ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
BOARD_X, BOARD_Y = 1, 1        # top-left corner (col, row)
BOARD_W, BOARD_H = 10, 5       # width and height in cells

# ── Colors ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EMPTY_COLOR  = (220, 225, 235)
WALL_COLOR   = (45,  50,  65)
FILL_COLOR   = (100, 180, 255)
START_COLOR  = (80,  200, 120)
SOURCE_COLOR = (220, 110,  60)
BTN_COLOR    = (80,  100, 130)

# ── Global state ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
walls       = set()    # set of (row, col) that are walls
wall_blocks = {}       # (row, col) -> WallBlock element
cells       = {}       # (row, col) -> Cell element  (filled in @viz block)
bfs_active  = False    # True after BFS runs; prevents re-clicking until cleared

# ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def in_board(row, col):
    return (BOARD_Y <= row < BOARD_Y + BOARD_H and
            BOARD_X <= col < BOARD_X + BOARD_W)

def do_bfs(start_r, start_c):
    global bfs_active
    bfs_active = True
    cells[(start_r, start_c)].set_fill(START_COLOR, 0)
    queue   = deque([(start_r, start_c, 0)])
    visited = {(start_r, start_c)}
    while queue:
        r, c, dist = queue.popleft()
        for dr, dc in [(-1, 0), (1, 0), (0, -1), (0, 1)]:
            nr, nc = r + dr, c + dc
            if (in_board(nr, nc)
                    and (nr, nc) not in visited
                    and (nr, nc) not in walls):
                visited.add((nr, nc))
                cells[(nr, nc)].set_fill(FILL_COLOR, dist + 1)
                queue.append((nr, nc, dist + 1))

# ── Element classes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# @viz

class Cell(Panel):
    """One board square: a background Rect + a Label for BFS distance."""
    def __init__(self, row, col):
        super().__init__(x=col, y=row, width=1, height=1)
        self._row = row
        self._col = col
        self._bg  = Rect(width=1, height=1, color=EMPTY_COLOR, z=5)
        self._lbl = Label(label='', width=1, height=1, font_size=11, z=4)
        self.add(self._bg, self._lbl)

    def on_click(self, x, y):
        if bfs_active or (self._row, self._col) in walls:
            return
        do_bfs(self._row, self._col)

    def set_fill(self, color, dist):
        self._bg.color   = color
        self._lbl.label  = str(dist)

    def reset(self):
        self._bg.color  = EMPTY_COLOR
        self._lbl.label = ''


class WallBlock(Rect):
    """A draggable wall tile. Overwrites the Cell's map entry when placed,
    making that cell draggable instead of BFS-clickable."""
    def __init__(self, x, y):
        super().__init__(x=x, y=y, width=1, height=1,
                         color=WALL_COLOR, z=0, animate=False)
        self._home       = (y, x)   # (row, col) of current board cell
        self._drag_start = None

    def on_click(self, x, y):
        pass   # absorb click so it does not fall through to the Cell

    def on_drag(self, x, y, drag_type):
        if drag_type == 'start':
            self._drag_start = self._home
            walls.discard(self._home)
            wall_blocks.pop(self._home, None)
            self.x = x
            self.y = y
        elif drag_type == 'mid':
            self.x = x
            self.y = y
        elif drag_type == 'end':
            r, c = y, x
            if in_board(r, c) and (r, c) not in walls:
                # Valid empty cell — place here
                self._home = (r, c)
                self.x = c
                self.y = r
                walls.add((r, c))
                wall_blocks[(r, c)] = self
            elif not in_board(r, c):
                # Dropped outside board — remove
                self.delete()
            else:
                # Occupied — snap back to where drag started
                sr, sc = self._drag_start
                self.x = sc
                self.y = sr
                self._home = self._drag_start
                walls.add(self._drag_start)
                wall_blocks[self._drag_start] = self


class SourceBlock(Rect):
    """Stays fixed outside the board. Dragging it spawns a new WallBlock
    that follows the mouse; releasing on a valid cell places it."""
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__(x=BOARD_X - 1, y=BOARD_Y + 2,
                         width=1, height=1, color=SOURCE_COLOR, z=0)
        self._spawned = None

    def on_drag(self, x, y, drag_type):
        if drag_type == 'start':
            self._spawned = WallBlock(x=x, y=y)
        elif drag_type == 'mid' and self._spawned:
            self._spawned.x = x
            self._spawned.y = y
        elif drag_type == 'end' and self._spawned:
            r, c = y, x
            if in_board(r, c) and (r, c) not in walls:
                self._spawned._home = (r, c)
                self._spawned.x = c
                self._spawned.y = r
                walls.add((r, c))
                wall_blocks[(r, c)] = self._spawned
            else:
                self._spawned.delete()
            self._spawned = None


class ClearBtn(Rect):
    def on_click(self, x, y):
        global bfs_active
        bfs_active = False
        for cell in cells.values():
            cell.reset()


# ── Build the board ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

for r in range(BOARD_Y, BOARD_Y + BOARD_H):
    for c in range(BOARD_X, BOARD_X + BOARD_W):
        cells[(r, c)] = Cell(r, c)

# Random initial walls (reproducible)
random.seed(42)
for r, c in random.sample(
        [(r, c) for r in range(BOARD_Y, BOARD_Y + BOARD_H)
                 for c in range(BOARD_X, BOARD_X + BOARD_W)],
        8):
    blk = WallBlock(x=c, y=r)
    walls.add((r, c))
    wall_blocks[(r, c)] = blk

# ── Source block (left of board) ──────────────────────────────────────────────
Label(label='drag\n+ wall', x=BOARD_X - 1, y=BOARD_Y,
      width=1, font_size=9, color=SOURCE_COLOR)
source = SourceBlock()

# ── Clear button (below board) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Label created before ClearBtn so ClearBtn wins the objects-map entry
Label(label='clear', x=BOARD_X, y=BOARD_Y + BOARD_H + 1,
      width=2, font_size=11)
clear_btn = ClearBtn(x=BOARD_X + 2, y=BOARD_Y + BOARD_H + 1,
                     width=3, height=1, color=BTN_COLOR)

# @end
